condition_context: {"applies_when": "residential mortgage lending forms a material proportion of the ADI's portfolio", "reviewer": "operationally independent and competent person"}
consequence: The risk management framework applicable to the residential mortgage lending portfolio must be comprehensively reviewed at least every three years, with results reported to the Board.

condition_context: {"applies_when": "material serviceability policies and procedures exist", "action": "review and update to align with changing external environment and risk appetite"}
consequence: Material serviceability policies and procedures must be reviewed at least annually and updated where required.

condition_context: {"applies_to": "all residential mortgage loans", "base_rate": "loan interest rate paid by borrower excluding introductory/honeymoon rates", "regulatory_basis": "Attachment C of APS 220"}
consequence: ADIs must apply a buffer of at least 3.0 percentage points over the loan's interest rate when assessing serviceability; failure to apply the buffer is a breach of APS 220.

condition_context: {"applies_to": "credit card or other revolving personal debt", "basis": "per month on total committed limit", "frequency": "monthly"}
consequence: ADIs should assess a borrower's repayment obligation for credit card or revolving personal debt using a rate of at least 3 per cent per month on the total committed limit when calculating borrower expenses.

condition_context: {"applies_when": "standard review cycle", "additional_trigger": "also reviewed when interest rates change"}
consequence: A prudent ADI should review its serviceability buffers and floors at least quarterly and whenever interest rates change.

condition_context: {"income_type": "most types of non-salary income including bonuses, overtime, variable commissions, investment income", "note": "higher discounts may be appropriate in some cases"}
consequence: A prudent ADI should apply a minimum discount of 20 per cent to most types of non-salary income when assessing borrower income for serviceability.

condition_context: {"income_type": "expected rental income on investment properties", "note": "larger haircuts appropriate for properties with higher risk of non-occupancy"}
consequence: Prudent serviceability policies should incorporate a minimum haircut of 20 per cent on expected rental income; larger haircuts apply where non-occupancy risk is higher.

condition_context: {"includes": "capitalised LMI premium or other fees", "classification": "high LVR lending threshold based on APRA experience"}
consequence: LVRs above 90 per cent (including capitalised LMI premium or other fees) clearly expose an ADI to a higher risk of loss; significant increases in such lending should trigger senior management review with Board oversight.
